name: Denis Leary
blurb: The Boston-Irish rant comic with a cigarette in one hand and a beer in the other, who turned MTV interstitials and one Showtime hour into the angriest stand-up brand of the 1990s.
bio: Denis Leary came up in Boston's Ding Ho scene alongside Steven Wright, Lenny Clarke, and Bobcat Goldthwait, then moved to London in 1988 and built a stage persona — chain-smoking, beer-swilling, perpetually furious at vegetarians, hippies, the over-medicated, and the chronically sensitive — that found its American audience through a run of MTV interstitials in 1992 and the 1993 Showtime special No Cure for Cancer. The album hit, the single Asshole reached MTV rotation and topped Triple J's Hottest 100 of 1993, and his 1997 HBO hour Lock 'N Load cemented him as a working stand-up before he pivoted to acting (Rescue Me, The Ref, Demolition Man) and to running the Leary Firefighters Foundation. Long-tail accusation that early material overlapped with Bill Hicks; Leary has acknowledged the friendship and denied the lift.

timeline.ev: Earns BFA from Emerson College; begins stand-up in Boston
Moves to London; develops the rant persona on the U.K. comedy circuit
MTV airs his series of 30-second comedic rants; Asshole single released and peaks on alt-rock radio
Showtime special No Cure for Cancer (Feb. 20); album hits gold; book published the same year
HBO special Lock 'N Load (directed by Ted Demme)
Founds the Leary Firefighters Foundation after the Worcester Cold Storage warehouse fire kills six firefighters including his cousin and a childhood friend
Co-creates and stars in Rescue Me on FX (2004–2011)
Comedy Central special Denis Leary's Merry F#%$in' Christmas
Comedy Central special Douchebags and Donuts (Jan. 16)

accolades: Asshole voted No. 1 on Triple J's Hottest 100 of 1993 (Australia)
Two-time Emmy nominee for Rescue Me (Lead Actor in a Comedy)
Founded the Leary Firefighters Foundation (2000) — has raised over $20 million for first-responder equipment and training
New York Times bestseller for Why We Suck (2008)
Four major stand-up specials between 1993 and 2011 across Showtime, HBO, and Comedy Central

ranking_notes: Asshole (UrgpZ0fUixs, DenisLearyVEVO) is the only fully verified-official Leary clip and is itself the canonical Leary bit — the song that made him an MTV name. Top-100 song-as-bit candidate.

No Cure for Cancer streams on Netflix (verified, 200); the bit-by-bit YouTube uploads (smoking, coffee, vegetarians) are all on non-official archive channels (Stringer's Stuff, TheComedyPop, ZANy comedY, Bumblebeemagnify) and were dropped per the official-channel gate. Worth checking again if HBO/Showtime/Comedy Central uploads any to their own channels.
Honest disclosure: bit count came in at 1 because Leary's catalog largely predates the era of official YouTube standup clip uploads, and the 1990s rights are scattered.
